biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — 29d7d6be-2 (cd+ls+find+wc-l+rg via Bash), 29d7d6be-3 (find+grep pipeline via Bash), 29d7d6be-6 (cat>> append via Bash instead of Write), 29d7d6be-24 (grep/sed via Bash multiple calls), 29d7d6be-25 (grep+sed+python3-file-write via Bash), 29d7d6be-31 (git mv compound chains via Bash with failures), 29d7d6be-34 (python3+rg via Bash), bc2715fa-2 (pwd+ls+jq+tail via Bash), bc2715fa-5 (cd+rg x4 via Bash), bc2715fa-7 (rg on nonexistent paths via Bash), bc2715fa-14 (ls+cat+/usr/bin/grep compounds via Bash), bc2715fa-18 (cat>> append to mistake-patterns.md via Bash instead of Write), bc2715fa-21 (sole tool is /usr/bin/grep), bc2715fa-22 (find+stash+git-diff via Bash), bc2715fa-29 (lsof+pm2 via Bash plus /usr/bin/grep x2), bc2715fa-30 (ps+lsof via Bash plus /usr/bin/grep x2); rate ~62% of measurable units, seventh consecutive cycle within 55-65% band with zero improvement trajectory; structural non-improvement confirmed across all 7 cycles with no downward pressure from repeated flagging

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:STABLE — 29d7d6be-24 (3 distinct Edit fail-read-succeed cycles across 3 different files within one unit); 29d7d6be-31 (git mv compound chain fail x3 consecutive with no state-read between attempts); bc2715fa-14 (with-server.ts edited success=false x3 consecutive plus sink-options.ts failure plus console-sink.ts x4 edits); bc2715fa-32 (page.tsx fail x2 before Read pivot); 4 units consistent with prior cycles; no worsening or improvement trajectory

python3_file_write_via_bash_bypass:RECURRING:CONFIRMED — 29d7d6be-25 (python3 inline script p.write_text() bulk-injecting frontmatter into 10 files, bypassing Write tool entirely); 29d7d6be-34 (second python3 inline script via Bash for B3/C5 frontmatter normalization across multiple doc files); same two instances as prior calibration cycles confirming this is not improving; bulk-efficiency justification partially mitigates but pattern is structurally recurring across every session with bulk-file operations

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:STABLE — bc2715fa-5 (cd frontend prefix on rg compounds fails 4 consecutive times before pwd+ls recovery); bc2715fa-7 (rg targets src/utils/app-init.utils.ts and src/utils/with-server.ts both fail success=false before ls pivot); recovery topology identical to prior cycles; count at 2 matches stable baseline

declared_ready_without_runtime_exercise:RECURRING:CONFIRMED — bc2715fa-14 (declared crab-sink split ready after ts:build+vitest without dev server exercise; self-correction ritual executed in bc2715fa-18 immediately after); self-correction within same session confirms pattern recognition is present but not pre-empting the violation

cross_references.note: No git push operations observed in this batch; approval-boundary violations not testable from these units

bc2715fa-18 appends to mistake-patterns.md via cat>> for declared-ready-without-runtime-exercise; bc2715fa-22 appends trusted-linter-reminder-without-diffing via cat>> both as new entries rather than checking for existing similar entries — the deduplication insight from 2026-05-08 wake cycle is not being applied to the memory-append operations themselves

recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations is now confirmed structurally non-improving across 7 consecutive cycles at the same 55-65% rate — this bias cannot be addressed by continued flagging; the root cause is habitual Bash-first pattern selection rather than deliberate tool choice; recommendation: architectural intervention via a stronger pre-tool-selection check rather than post-hoc pattern recording

optimistic_edit_without_read has plateaued at 11 sub-instances rather than worsening further; however the unit count remains elevated at 6 — the failure mode is a direct pre-condition check that can be enforced: before any Edit call, verify a Read of the same target occurred in the same unit; this is the one bias with a clear mechanical prevention path

hook_bypass_via_absolute_grep_path structural gap persists: the hook does not intercept absolute-path grep invocations; this is an infrastructure fix not a behavioral one; the behavioral pattern (using /usr/bin/grep) continues in bc2715fa units despite the rule being codified; until the hook is patched, agent-side detection must substitute

re_edit_thrash and optimistic_edit_without_read share a root cause: both arise from editing without first verifying the target string exists in the file; a unified pre-edit-read enforcement rule would address both simultaneously; this merits consolidation in the atone system rather than tracking as two separate patterns
